claim: PopCorn is presented as a next-gen storyboard generator that defines framing, motion, and characters.
relevance: Direct basis for Phase 1 storyboard/shot board.

claim: Public page shows output of eight scenes, 16:9 aspect ratio, and high quality settings.
relevance: Supports shot-card board defaults and eight-shot starter workflow.

claim: FAQ says PopCorn can generate up to eight images in one sequence.
relevance: Supports small sequence storyboard rather than full timeline.

claim: FAQ says the final image can be used as a new reference input to continue the story.
relevance: Direct support for first/last-frame chaining.

claim: FAQ says finalized storyboards can be exported to Sora 2.
relevance: Shows storyboard as production handoff artifact; exact export mechanics need login validation.

claim: UI examples include Genre, Style, Camera, Character, Prompt, Camera Style, and Location.
relevance: Supports per-shot camera/settings data model.

claim: Page describes camera moves, locations, characters, moods, cast characters, locations and props, color and camera controls.
relevance: Supports shot-detail panel controls.

claim: FAQ says reusable Elements are Characters, Locations, and Props, callable with @tags.
relevance: Direct basis for References and @tag UX.

claim: FAQ says Cinema Studio supports all image/video models in one workspace.
relevance: Supports model catalog/variant comparison; exact availability changes over time.
status_notes: Confirmed FAQ but model list temporal

claim: Page describes script/brief to long multi-shot video with storyboard and references guiding flow.
relevance: Basis for Phase 2 script-to-board helper.

claim: Page lists multi-shot continuity, per-shot camera control, scene extension, and first/last-frame transitions.
relevance: Direct basis for continuity metadata and Phase 2 camera controls.

claim: Page describes reference system/character lock and up to 12 reference inputs per scene.
relevance: Useful model for References; exact per-model limits need validation.

claim: Page describes switching models per scene and comparing outputs side-by-side.
relevance: Direct basis for Variant compare grid.

claim: Soul ID creates consistent digital characters from photos; current page says minimum 20 photos and training around minutes.
relevance: Explains why full identity training is out-of-scope for OGA V1.

claim: Character page says users upload multiple-angle photos to train a character for repeated image/video use.
relevance: Supports CharacterBible concept but not training implementation.

claim: Soul Cast creates AI actors from genre/era/archetype/physique/outfit/imperfections and casting sheet/backstory.
relevance: Inspires CharacterBible fields; skip full synthetic actor generator.

claim: 2026 blog says Soul ID uses 20+ photos and avoids re-uploading a reference per shot.
relevance: Shows training-based identity consistency is a separate product class.

claim: 2026 blog says Soul ID handles who, while Cinema Studio handles how the camera moves.
relevance: Supports splitting CharacterBible from camera controls in OGA.

claim: Canvas is described as node-based/infinite workspace where prompts/images/references become workflow nodes.
relevance: Future Workflow Studio inspiration; not Phase 1.

claim: FAQ says prior generations/assets can be dropped in as nodes and workflows shared/collaborated on.
relevance: Supports 'outputs become inputs' principle.

claim: Collab includes shared projects, chat/calls, sharing generations with prompt/model/preview context.
relevance: Future share/audit inspiration; not Phase 1.

claim: MCP page says agent clients can generate images/videos, train characters, browse history, and reuse prior generations after account auth.
relevance: Future automation inspiration; not Phase 1.

claim: A user reports difficulty using saved Soul Characters inside PopCorn.
relevance: Signals integration friction; needs login validation.

claim: Third-party article claims manual/auto modes, multiple image references, and bridge-frame chaining.
relevance: Good validation questions; not treated as confirmed product truth.

claim: Repo describes an open-source AI image/video/cinema/lip-sync studio.
relevance: Confirms target product category.

claim: Package is MIT and workspace-based.
relevance: Supports additive OSS-friendly planning.

claim: Releases mention current model/tooling additions such as audio/video/design related features.
relevance: Signals fast-moving repo; planner should verify latest surfaces.

claim: Storyboarder visualizes stories and animatics.
relevance: UX pattern for shot boards.

claim: Older post says Storyboarder would be MIT licensed with exceptions.
relevance: License must be verified before direct code reuse.

claim: Zou stores production data such as projects, shots, assets, tasks, file metadata and preview files; AGPL-3.0.
relevance: Great data-model inspiration; avoid direct code if AGPL incompatible.

claim: OTIO represents editorial timing/clip metadata and references external media rather than containing media.
relevance: Strong support for metadata-first design.

claim: SwarmUI is MIT and has grid-generation/parameter comparison patterns.
relevance: Useful variant compare UX idea.

claim: Apache-2.0 TypeScript/Node/Electron app for text-to-character/storyboard/video workflow with infinite canvas and agents.
relevance: Closest stack-fit prior art; study architecture and UX.

claim: Next.js app for script/novel parsing, characters/scenes/storyboards/video, but license clarity and maturity need review.
relevance: Copy ideas only unless license audited.

claim: MIT multi-agent script-to-video framework with storyboard/shot list/key frames/first-last-frame pipeline.
relevance: Useful script-to-board and continuity pipeline inspiration.

claim: MIT React/Svelte node UI libraries.
relevance: Possible future Canvas/Workflow fit; not required for shot board.

claim: Production/commercial use has a specific license model; not plain OSS for every use.
relevance: Avoid embedding in V1 without license plan.

claim: Prompt management centralizes, versions, and tests prompts.
relevance: Inspiration for PromptTemplate versioning.

claim: Generated-image metadata/provenance can be read back from images.
relevance: Supports prompt/settings provenance principle.
